items.title: New watchdog proposed to probe behaviour of WA judges

children.content: Complaints about the behaviour and capacity of judges and magistrates will be investigated by an independent watchdog, under new legislation to be introduced to WA parliament this week.

gallery.altText: A judge in black robes writes on white paper in a court room with a gavel in front of them.

thumbnail.altText: A judge in black robes writes on white paper in a court room with a gavel in front of them.

items.title: Police officer in custody after Perth girl shot dead overseas

children.content: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.

image.altText: A silhouette of two Pakistani police officers holding rifles at a barrier or gate of some sort.

items.title: Former potato farm given new life as bird sanctuary

children.content: Once drained for farm irrigation, this former potato farm now attracts birds from as far away as Siberia.

items.title: Soccer tournament unites refugees through universal language of sport

children.content: A soccer tournament in Perth's north helps participants overcome barriers, while inspiring more women to pursue the game, as part of Refugee Week.

gallery.altText: A woman wears a red jumper and holds a soccer ball out to the camera.
A team huddles on a soccer field.
Ladies with head scarves watch a soccer match.

thumbnail.altText: A woman wears a red jumper and holds a soccer ball out to the camera.
A team huddles on a soccer field.
Ladies with head scarves watch a soccer match.

items.title: People delight in finding wildflowers all year round in WA

children.content: WA has a reputation for putting on incredible wildflower displays, with those on the ground saying you can find something special all year round.

image.altText: A close of a purple flower

items.title: Woman ditches custom wedding gown for $90 op shop dream dress

children.content: Kaila Scally-Chadwick abandoned her previously purchased made-to-order gown when she found her dream dress in a second-hand shop just months out from the big day.

image.altText: Baby wearing white standing holding the train of a lace wedding dress and looking at the camera with mum crouched beside

items.title: Locations released for 18 community batteries in Perth, Bunbury

children.content: Construction begins on 18 community batteries across WA, as a climate and conservation advocate calls for a legislated target for renewable energy uptake.

gallery.altText: A community battery is decorated with green artwork
Three people walk past a community battery painted with green artwork
Josh Wilson stands in front of a community battery with green artwork on it

Three people walk past a community battery painted with green artwork
Josh Wilson stands in front of a community battery with green artwork on it

items.title: 'Seeking connection': The unseen children who roam the streets at night

children.content: Youth Beat workers take to Perth streets at night to help young people who have nowhere else to go.

gallery.altText: Two teenage girls walk between large brick pylons in Perth's CBD at night.
Two youth workers chat to two young women sitting on a pavement.
Two women who are youth workers stand in a Perth street at night talking to a teenage girl in a red jumper.

thumbnail.altText: Two teenage girls walk between large brick pylons in Perth's CBD at night.
Two youth workers chat to two young women sitting on a pavement.
Two women who are youth workers stand in a Perth street at night talking to a teenage girl in a red jumper.

items.title: Discovery of woman's body in WA's Goldfields sparks homicide investigation

children.content: Police are investigating the death of a woman whose body was discovered on the outskirts of Kalgoorlie-Boulder.

image.altText: A police car parked at the side of the road with green scrub in the foreground and large trees in the background.

items.title: These ocean users have different ways of protecting themselves from sharks

children.content: After the deaths of three spearfishers from shark attacks, the conversation has turned to which shark deterrent measures are in place across the country and which ones have been found to work well.

image.altText: A man with short dark and grey hair, wearing a black t-shirt, standing in a workshop.

items.title: Why older Australians with MND are considering early euthanasia

children.content: The death of AFL legend Neale Daniher has shone a light on the grim choices faced by older Australians fighting motor neurone disease.

image.altText: a man who is paralysed lying in a bed with his neck at an angle

items.title: Scotland claims first World Cup win in 36 years as Brazil, Morocco share spoils

children.content: Brazil and Morocco share the spoils in a blockbuster clash after Qatar salvaged a late draw against Switzerland in San Francisco, while Scotland hung on to beat Haiti.

image.altText: John McGinn puts his hands over his eyes like Birdman in celebration of a goal

items.title: Meet the Brisbane couple who have donated more than $100 million to charity

children.content: Quentin and Kylie Birt say they were inspired to donate at the Big Freeze event after reading Neale Daniher's book.

image.altText: A woman and man in hiking gear surrounded by trees.

items.title: Here's what you need to know about the KPMG scandal

children.content: Accounting firm KPMG is engulfed in a scandal that has seen several senior leaders quit and ASIC launch an investigation into the firm's business dealings.

image.altText: A large, grey building with a blue and white sign at the top reading KPMG.

items.title: Why this Socceroos squad is like no other

children.content: The Socceroos squad is like no other that's ever been sent to represent us at a World Cup. See the extraordinary journeys that forged a team to represent modern Australia on the world stage.

articles.title: When and how to watch the Socceroos first World Cup match today

children.content: The Socceroos will face Türkiye in their first Group D match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Soccer player poses with a flexted arm, and pointing down the camera

articles.title: What to know if you're jumping on the Socceroos bandwagon

children.content: The world's biggest sporting event is back on our screens, and Australia is just hours away from taking the pitch. Here's a quick guide to get you across the basics.

image.altText: A soccer player holds his hand in the air.

items.title: Ice hockey drawing larger crowds with the help of shows like Heated Rivalry

children.content: It started with just three teams, but Australia's ice hockey players say the sport's speed and energy are drawing bigger crowds.

image.altText: Four hockey players on an ice rink celebrating while a crowd watches.

items.title: Senior Labor figures weigh in on BHP strike, cost could be $120m per day

children.content: Federal Resources Minister Madeleine King says workers threatening strike action at the nation's biggest port deserve "every single cent" of what they earn while a peak industry body says they are "letting down everyone".

image.altText: Upwards shot of the BHP building in Perth.

items.title: Why the swimming pool is an oasis in these outback communities

children.content: In Kalumburu and Balgo, two of Western Australia's most isolated towns, investing in a community swimming pool has been a boon for everyone.

gallery.altText: Aboriginal boy jumping into the pool
Children play in Ngukurr's public pool
Indigenous woman with grey hair sitting with children

thumbnail.altText: Aboriginal boy jumping into the pool
Children play in Ngukurr's public pool
Indigenous woman with grey hair sitting with children

items.title: Expert medical report on Floreat murderer sought ahead of inquest

children.content: The main focus of a coronial inquest into the death of a Perth mother and her daughter will be the medical care their killer Mark Bombara received in the lead up to the double-murder suicide, a coroner reveals.

image.altText: Jennifer and Gretl Petelczyc who were shot dead in their own home by Mark James Bombara.

items.title: Killers of Greenbushes man show no remorse during sentencing

children.content: A judge said the men, who were convicted of killing 68-year-old Raymond Smith during a drug-fuelled home invasion in the WA town of Greenbushes, showed no remorse.

image.altText: An older man with a long pony tail pats a big dog.

items.title: New line of hope in discovering the Nannup Four

children.content: The vice-president of a search team that solved two high-profile missing persons cases in Tasmania turns his attention to the Nannup Four case after it was featured in the Expanse podcast.

image.altText: A photo of two men, a woman and a girl with a photo of bushland in the background.

items.title: After 70 bags of fruit were stolen in one weekend, farmer closes roadside stall

children.content: Mandarin grower Bruce Crain says people's growing dishonesty, including the theft of 70 bags in one weekend, makes it impossible to offer a roadside service.

image.altText: Several bags of mandarins on a black table with a sign reading "$5 per bag" placed inside of a corrugated steel shelter.

items.title: Andrew Forrest defends native title deals after $150m Yindjibarndi court loss

children.content: Mining magnate Andrew "Twiggy" Forrest has defended his company's approach to Indigenous agreements after a court ordered the miner to pay $150 million in compensation to the Yindjibarndi people.

gallery.altText: Andrew Forrest stands at the front of an auditorium holding a microphone and wearing a beige suit jacket.

thumbnail.altText: Andrew Forrest stands at the front of an auditorium holding a microphone and wearing a beige suit jacket.
